patterns_recognized: 7/21 mandate: lean CONFIRM on GOLD unless the suppression rule has ≥70% recent track record on that exact shape. Two consecutive CHW@TEX false negatives (7/20 OVERRIDE lost 10-3, then 7/21 DOWNGRADE missed 10-run TEX win) are the definitive evidence. DOWNGRADEing CHW@TEX a third straight time on the same non-validated rationale directly violates this mandate.

CAP10-rescue went 1-for-3 on 7/21 real stakes; the two losses (SF@KC F3=822.2, DET@CHC) had profiles similar to today's SF@KC (F3=735.3, edge<1pp, demoted tier). F3>1000 remains the preferred rescue qualifier — today's SF@KC does not clear the bar.

CAP7 sub-0.02 band (gap ≤ 0.02) is the documented false-negative zone from 7/10–7/19 (4 consecutive misses). Gap > 0.02 is a legitimate fire. CHW@TEX gap=+0.006 is inside the false-negative band (CONFIRM per mandate); MIN@CLE gap=+0.0254 and MIA@HOU gap=+0.0205 are above 0.02 (legitimate fires).

F3_swing_take_gap < 1000 is a validated DOWNGRADE signal independent of the CAP7 sub-0.02 debate. Validated 7/21: NYM@MIL F3=243.9 correctly DOWNGRADEd (shutout 4-0). Applies today to NYM@MIL (F3=646.4) and SF@KC (F3=735.3).
D-grade dual-opposing (SP-xERA + PQI both against pick) continues as reliable stake-suppression-only across OAK@ARI, CIN@SEA, LAD@PHI, TB@TOR, and DET@CHC today — never a directional fade.

PIT @ NYY G1.reasoning: CAP1 fired on edge=-4.41pp and correctly demoted the pre-cap A- (score=4) to B- (score=1). Cole's 1.51 xERA gap and PQI Δ=+18.3 confirm NYY's directional edge, but negative edge means the market has priced out our advantage. CAP1 is non-negotiable (validated ~80-85% precision); the cap already handled suppression so no additional DOWNGRADE is warranted. Stake is zero regardless; direction is NYY.

PIT @ NYY G2.reasoning: Two independent suppressors already stack: (1) fair_prob missing — Odds API did not fire, so staking without a closing-line anchor is not permissible; (2) CAP7 fires at gap=+0.0216, above the 0.02 legitimate-fire threshold (not in the false-negative band). F1*=1.31 (Chandler thin sample) without F3 would ordinarily prompt a DOWNGRADE, but the two prior suppressors make it moot. Fried's advantage is real; game is unstakeable. Stake is zero.

BAL @ BOS G1.reasoning: Score=2 (B) with only PQI confirmation (Δ=+21.6) and Stage agreement — no F-signal fired, no SP-xERA edge in the grade stack. Edge=6.19pp is in the valid window but a two-signal B stack never earns GOLD under current rules. Bennett (BOS) vs Kremer (BAL away) is directionally BOS-favorable but lacks the independent corroboration for GOLD. All BvP is null (no prior matchup history). CONFIRM SKIP.

SF @ KC.reasoning: Not rescuing this CAP10 bottom-bucket pick (pick_prob=0.546). F3=735.3 is below the 1000 meaningful-anchor threshold — the same rescue disqualifier that validated the 7/21 NYM@MIL DOWNGRADE. Edge is 0.65pp (outside the 4-15pp valid window). Yesterday SF@KC was a CAP10-rescue CONFIRM that lost 3-2 on F3=822.2 (also sub-1000). The DIAMOND→GOLD demotion adds a third suppressor. No rescue.

NYM @ MIL.reasoning: CAP7 fires at gap=+0.0001 (deepest false-negative band value seen yet), but F3=646.4 is below the 1000 meaningful-anchor threshold — the same driver that correctly called the 7/21 NYM@MIL DOWNGRADE (F3=243.9, MIL shutout 4-0). The sub-1000 F3 DOWNGRADE rule has clean archive validation independent of the disputed CAP7 threshold debate. Edge barely inside the window at 4.25pp. DOWNGRADE on F3-anchor grounds.

WSH @ COL.reasoning: F2_xwoba_gap=0.025 and F5_bullpen_gap=+1.07 provide two-dimensional F-signal support with no starred F1 concern today. The 7/20 lesson specifically validated F2+F5 secondary support as corroboration: both BAL@BOS and WSH@COL missed wins that day when DOWNGRADEd without F3. Edge=5.65pp is in the valid window. James Wood (xwoba 0.425, order 1) anchors WSH's offense. Lean-CONFIRM mandate applies; no suppressor has ≥70% track record against this shape.

OAK @ ARI.reasoning: D-grade (-3) with SP edge 3.67 xERA AGAINST ARI pick (Gage Jump/OAK has a massive advantage over Merrill Kelly/ARI), PQI Δ=-28.1 against ARI, Stage 1/2 disagree (Δ=0.12), confidence_downgrade=True, and bullpen_fatigue. The prob_ceiling_0.70 flag fired (HARD CAP 9 zone). The model picked ARI while virtually every quantitative signal opposes it. CONFIRM SKIP — no directional fade toward OAK inferred.

CIN @ SEA.reasoning: fair_prob=0.3727 is below the 0.42 viability floor — the archive's most reliable single suppressor. Additionally: SP edge 0.87 xERA AGAINST CIN (Hancock/SEA has the advantage), PQI Δ=+18.4 against CIN, and f5_full_delta=0.23 (major Stage 1/2 disagreement). SEA's Arozarena (shrunk 0.585, 14 PA) and Naylor (shrunk 0.545, 28 PA) are both WEAK_VS vs Singer — a mild positive for CIN — but the structural suppressors overwhelm. CONFIRM SKIP.

STL @ LAA.reasoning: Edge 2.33pp below the 4pp floor. f5_full_delta=0.2675 reflects genuine model uncertainty (Stage 1 says STL wins F5 with ~85%; Stage 2 flips to LAA 57.9% full game on bullpen leverage). The PQI>+30 + SP-edge>1.5 carve-out threshold proposed after the 7/21 STL@LAA mandate is not met today: PQI=+17.9 falls below +30. Two consecutive suppressed LAA wins (7/20, 7/21) reached the evaluation threshold, but today's configuration does not qualify. CONFIRM SKIP.

MIN @ CLE.reasoning: CAP7 fires at gap=+0.0254 — above the 0.02 threshold, a legitimate cap fire (not the sub-0.02 false-negative band). CAP10 also fires independently on pick_prob=0.536 in the bottom bucket. MIN's SP edge is only 0.31 xERA (very thin). CLE's BvP shows José Ramírez as OWNER vs Ober (32 PA, shrunk OPS 0.871, OWNER flag) — a meaningful risk for MIN's starter. Both caps apply legitimately; no rescue case exists.

LAD @ PHI.reasoning: Score=-4 (D): SP edge 0.77 xERA AGAINST LAD (Nola/PHI has the advantage over Lauer/LAD), PQI Δ=+7.6 against LAD, and f5_full_delta=0.6086 — today's largest Stage 1/2 split by a wide margin. Edge=15.75pp above the 15pp ceiling. LAD's full-game pick depends entirely on bullpen leverage reversing a Nola-dominated F5. Trea Turner is OWNER vs Lauer (17 PA, shrunk 0.903, OWNER) — a concrete PHI risk. CONFIRM SKIP.

TB @ TOR.reasoning: D-grade (-2): SP edge 1.56 xERA AGAINST the TB pick (Fisher/TOR has the advantage over Jax/TB), PQI Δ=+21.1 against TB. Edge 3.73pp below the 4pp floor. Today's signal profile differs structurally from 7/21's TB@TOR (Gausman F1* pre-cap A — the game that missed a 10-run win on a flat-edge DOWNGRADE): today TB's own SP Jax faces the superior Fisher with two opposing signals. CONFIRM SKIP on a clean dual-opposing D-grade.

SD @ ATL.reasoning: Edge 1.76pp below the 4pp floor; confidence_downgrade=True. CAP10 fired on pick_prob=0.539 in the bottom bucket. Score=2 (B) reflects only thin PQI +5.6 and Stage agreement — insufficient for GOLD or rescue. Martín Pérez (ATL home SP) faces Manny Machado who is OWNER vs Pérez (27 PA, shrunk OPS 0.855, OPS 1.005 raw) — a concrete qualitative risk for ATL's pick-side SP. Multiple suppressors; no qualifying rescue signal.

CHW @ TEX.reasoning: The 7/21 postgame applies directly: DOWNGRADEing this exact matchup (same CHW@TEX with Alexander/Kay) for a second consecutive day on a blanket same-prior-day rule was explicitly identified as a process failure that missed a 10-run TEX win. The lean-CONFIRM mandate requires ≥70% validated suppressor — F1* rule is 4W/2L (67%, below threshold), and CAP7 at gap=+0.006 is inside the documented sub-0.02 false-negative band. Pre-cap score=5 (A) with PQI Δ=+27.8 (today's strongest confirmation) and SP edge 2.13 xERA confirm TEX. CONFIRM.

DET @ CHC.reasoning: D-grade (-2): SP edge 1.97 xERA AGAINST the CHC pick (Montero/DET has the xERA advantage over Rea/CHC), PQI Δ=-22.2 against CHC. Yesterday CHC won 11-2 in this series with DET as the GOLD pick — today the model has flipped to CHC while both SP and PQI oppose the new pick. Bullpen_fatigue flag adds further caution. CONFIRM SKIP — yesterday's blowout does not override today's opposing-signal suppression.

MIA @ HOU.reasoning: F3=1528.3 (above 1000) and F5=+1.10 are genuine positives, but CAP7 fires at gap=+0.0205 — above the 0.02 threshold, a legitimate fire (not the false-negative band). Edge 2.92pp is below the 4pp floor. PQI Δ=+4.5 is very thin confirmation. Sandy Alcantara (MIA away SP) is an elite arm opposing a thin HOU SP edge of 0.41 xERA. PLATINUM→GOLD demotion adds a suppressor. Between sub-floor edge, legitimate CAP7, thin PQI, and Alcantara quality, SKIP is the correct tier.

BAL @ BOS G2.reasoning: BOS home SP not yet announced; pipeline correctly withheld a directional pick until both starters meet the 100+ Statcast pitch minimum. This is likely the rescheduled 7/21 postponed BAL@BOS game, which had a pre-cap A CONFIRM pending validation. No directional call can be made without valid SP data. CONFIRM the data gate.
